title: INGLEWOOD, CA

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claim_texts": [
    "Assembly Bill 1866 mandates that companies in California meet targets to plug an estimated 40,000 idle oil wells.",
    "The bill requires larger oil operators to plug 15% of their idle wells in the first year, increasing to 20% annually by 2030.",
    "Operators are required to prioritize plugging wells within 3,200 feet of where communities live, work, and play.",
    "Leaks from idle oil wells cause air and water pollution, threatening public health, the environment, and the climate.",
    "The burden of cleaning up idle oil wells should not fall on taxpayers.",
    "AB 1866 is expected to create union jobs while ensuring polluters are held responsible for cleanup.",
    "Communities that have suffered from oil and gas pollution will benefit from reduced pollution and improved health and safety.",
    "The oil industry's practices have allowed dangerous idle oil and gas wells to remain unplugged across California.",
    "The existing taxpayer liability for orphan wells in California exceeds $1 billion.",
    "California taxpayers have already spent $100 million in recent years to plug orphan wells.",
    "Plugging idle oil wells could create thousands of skilled jobs statewide.",
    "Idle oil wells in California are a major source of methane gas emissions and air pollution.",
    "About two-thirds of idle oil wells are estimated to be leaking methane, a climate super-pollutant significantly stronger than carbon dioxide."
  ]
}
```
